reasoning_content:
We need to determine if 4957 is prime. Check divisibility by small primes. 

First, note square root of 4957 is about sqrt(4957) ≈ 70.4 (since 70^2=4900, 71^2=5041). So we need to test primes up to 70.

Check divisibility by 2: no, odd.
3: sum digits 4+9+5+7=25, not divisible by 3.
5: last digit not 0 or 5.
7: 7*708=4956, so remainder 1, not divisible.
11: alternating sum: 4-9+5-7=-7, not multiple of 11.
13: 13*381=4953, remainder 4, so no.
17: 17*291=4947, remainder 10; 17*292=4964 too high, so no.
19: 19*261=4959, too high; 19*260=4940, remainder 17, so no.
23: 23*215=4945, remainder 12; 23*216=4968, no.
29: 29*171=4959, too high; 29*170=4930, remainder 27, no.
31: 31*160=4960, too high; 31*159=4929, remainder 28, no.
37: 37*134=4958, remainder -1, so 37*134=4958, so 4957 not divisible; 37*133=4921, remainder 36.
41: 41*121=4961, too high; 41*120=4920, remainder 37, no.
43: 43*115=4945, remainder 12; 43*116=4988, no.
47: 47*105=4935, remainder 22; 47*106=4982, no.
53: 53*93=4929, remainder 28; 53*94=4982, no.
59: 59*84=4956, remainder 1, so no.
61: 61*81=4941, remainder 16; 61*82=5002, no.
67: 67*74=4958, remainder -1, so not divisible; 67*73=4891, remainder 66.
71: already > sqrt, so stop.

Thus no divisors found, so 4957 is prime. Answer: yes.
